title: scorzoneroides autumnalis subsp. pratensis
intro: Scorzoneroides autumnalis subsp. pratensis, also known as autumn knapweed, is a plant species within the sunflower family that is native to Europe. It is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, and its health benefits are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Autumn knapweed is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
